message:
fuzz(block_aligned_vec): new differential fuzz target with 10 seeds

Add fuzz_block_aligned_vec, a new libfuzzer target exercising the
ffs_block::AlignedVec wrapper used throughout the block-IO path.
The target is structure-aware: it consumes the fuzzer-supplied
bytes through a small ByteCursor and builds a sequence of
AlignedVec operations against a parallel Vec<u8> shadow model,
asserting each operation preserves three invariants:

1. as_slice() == shadow model (content equality)
2. len()/is_empty() match the model
3. alignment() matches the requested alignment, normalized via
   normalized_alignment() (rounded up to the next power of two,
   capped at MAX_ALIGNMENT_POWER = 8192, with 0/1 → 1)

Plus an address-level check: when the vec is non-empty,
as_slice().as_ptr().addr() is a multiple of the expected alignment.
That verifies AlignedVec actually delivers on its alignment contract,
not just bookkeeps it.

Operations covered include AlignedVec::new(len, alignment),
AlignedVec::from_vec(payload, alignment), in-place mutate of the
underlying slice, replace, and clone-after-mutate (verifies the
shadow stays correct even when an existing vec is replaced).

Sized to stay within libFuzzer's per-input budget:
- MAX_INPUT_BYTES   = 2048
- MAX_VEC_LEN       =  512
- MAX_ALIGNMENT_REQ = 8192

Ten hand-curated structured seeds prime the corpus, each named
for the operation/edge case it covers:

- seed_empty_default            — zero-length default constructor
- seed_new_len_one_align_zero   — len=1 with alignment request=0
- seed_new_len_64_align_4096    — typical 4 KiB-page alignment
- seed_zero_len_high_alignment  — empty vec + page-sized alignment
- seed_large_alignment_request  — alignment near MAX_ALIGNMENT_POWER
- seed_from_vec_unaligned_request — from_vec with non-power-of-two
- seed_into_vec_roundtrip       — into_vec / from_vec round-trip
- seed_mutate_middle            — interior mutate via as_mut_slice
- seed_many_replacements        — sequence of replace operations
- seed_clone_after_mutate       — clone preserves model after mutate

Wire the target into fuzz/Cargo.toml as a new [[bin]] entry next to
fuzz_block_mem_io_engine so `cargo +nightly fuzz run
fuzz_block_aligned_vec` works out of the box on a clean checkout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -89,6 +89,11 @@ name = "fuzz_block_mem_io_engine"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_block_mem_io_engine.rs"
 doc = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_block_aligned_vec"
+path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_block_aligned_vec.rs"
+doc = false
+
 [[bin]]
 name = "fuzz_ext4_xattr"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_ext4_xattr.rs"
